methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $315M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Lilac Solutions is a Series C-stage climate and energy technology company that has raised $315M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of three same-sector, same-stage private peers (KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M) alongside one relevant public comparable, supplemented by a broader peer set of eight private companies including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis uses total capital raised as a baseline proxy of $315M and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $536M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables establishes a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B. These approaches produce meaningfully different signals, reflecting heterogeneity in capital intensity and market reception within the climate tech sector.

The triangulated range supports a valuation between $455M (low) and $1.1B (high), with a median of $768M. The 2.4x spread between low and high reflects material uncertainty around whether Lilac's technology and execution track more closely with earlier-stage climate companies or the premium-valuation peers in the comparable set. Capital efficiency relative to peers and progress toward commercial deployment remain key variables in narrowing this range.
